content: The built-in Tag feature doesn't provide for entering dates; it's only for applying a color to the message by selecting the color. Each color is numbered. If you did in fact mean to say number instead of date, you can undo the tag by clicking the message in the message list, then press the zero key.

content:
What, exactly, happens when 'it doesn't work'? Please quote any and all error messages you see, completely and verbatim. Screen shots would be OK.


I hate to complicate matters, but blame Yahoo. Apparently they have introduced a new twist to authentication that affects some Thunderbird (and other email agents, presumably) users. I have multiple Yahoo accounts, but I have not been bitten by this yet, but I expect I will be eventually, probably if/when I am forced to migrate from Comcast.

Here is what Thunderbird has to say about it:

https://support.mozilla.org/en-US/kb/thunderbird-and-yahoo

If you have more than one Yahoo account, this part may be important:

If you have more than one account, such as one AOL account and one Yahoo account, to allow PKCE to work you must:

  1. Click ≡ > Settings > General.
  2. Scroll down to the bottom and click Config Editor...
  3. Click "Accept the risk and continue" if that appears.
  4. Type mailnews.oauth.usePrivateBrowser into the search box.
  5. Click on the Toggle (double arrow) button to change the value to true.
  6. Quit Thunderbird and then restart it.
  7. Retry logging in to your accounts (Yahoo, ATT, AOL).

content:
Ok, try this

  1. Assuming you are at the folder where the trash file is, EXIT thunderbird if it is running:
  2. Open the file "trash" with Notepad or a similar text editor. - you probably need to highlight the file, rightclick and select 'open with' and select notepad
  3. Locate the line before your email text that reads "X-Mozilla-Status: 000X" (where 'X' is any character other than 0). Change the number to four zeros (″0000"). Use Edit>find in Notepad to search for next occurrence of 'x-mozilla-status"' and repeat. Keep doing that until you have changed all messages to the 0000 status. Save the document and exit Notepad.) - see attached screenshot for example of how it looks.)
  4. now, COPY the trash file to the Mail\Local Folders folder and rename to something other than trash.
  5. restart thunderbird and the messages should appear.

Let me know how it goes. This technique works with other folders, but I've never tried it with the Trash folder.

content:
It's not clear to me from your description whether you use Thunderbird to interface with your Yahoo mail account, or if you have a separate app for Yahoo, or you use Yahoo web mail.

Yahoo is correct, the "new mail" sound for Thunderbird is local to your computer. Out of the box, Thunderbird will use the default system sound, which might clash with a separate program trying to use that sound at the same time. You can configure Thunderbird to use a different sound. See <Settings | General | Incoming | Play a sound | Use the following sound file...>.

A Yahoo app or web page would have it's own sound or might use the default system sound, which can also be changed in the operating system settings.
